danger_signals.visual_form: captain unit on map with opposing allegiance indicator
danger_signals.meaning: hostile military leadership; threat to player control; must be eliminated to win

danger_signals.visual_form: grouped soldiers with enemy allegiance color/marking
danger_signals.meaning: military threat; potential combat engagement required

danger_signals.visual_form: balance scale below mini-map tilting toward opponent
danger_signals.meaning: world-state deteriorating; enemy gaining territorial/political control

danger_signals.visual_form: isolated captain unit with command lag indicator (homing pigeon animation)
danger_signals.meaning: vulnerable command structure; delayed response to orders increases danger

danger_signals.visual_form: low food resource indicator in controlled towns
danger_signals.meaning: army starvation risk; loss of manpower and control potential

safety_signals.visual_form: town under player allegiance with local population indicator
safety_signals.meaning: secure resource base; can draft soldiers and manufacture equipment

safety_signals.visual_form: player's main character or subordinate captain on map
safety_signals.meaning: maintains army cohesion; loss means disbandment of forces

safety_signals.visual_form: high food supply in friendly towns or slaughtered livestock availability
safety_signals.meaning: army sustainability; enables sustained military operations

safety_signals.visual_form: balance scale below mini-map tilting heavily toward player side
safety_signals.meaning: political/military dominance; path to map victory

safety_signals.visual_form: soldiers carrying weapons (bows, swords, pikes) in army formation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: opposing factions display contrasting color palette
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: player-controlled towns show allegiance through building/population state

faction_indicators.color_identity: distinct from player color; multiple enemy factions may use different hues
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: captain units visually distinct from player captains through color/uniform
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: enemy-controlled towns display hostile visual state; soldiers show enemy affiliation

faction_indicators.color_identity: neutral color palette (likely grey or brown)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: neutral captains exist in some larger towns; visually distinguished from allegiant captains
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: towns with no active allegiance display independent visual state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: visual reduction of tree sprites in affected regions; environmental barrenness increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: weather pattern change; increased rain or snow fall (depending on season) causes slower unit movement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: map terrain and vegetation display seasonal color shifts (implied by weather/snow references)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: movement speed affected by weather; season determines precipitation type and intensity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: town color allegiance changes; soldier population indicators shift; power balance scale moves
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: town locals become available for drafting; townspeople transition to military or production labor

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: captain unit disappears from map; army loses command indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: surviving soldiers disband and return to hometown; army cohesion collapses; control reverts to town

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: soldiers display equipped weapons (bows, swords, pikes) in hand; captain carries excess equipment load
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: weapon distribution follows priority hierarchy; equipment quality affects combat effectiveness

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: army stance indicator (passive, neutral, aggressive) affects how units display behavior
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: posture influences resource collection speed and item manufacturing priority; aggressive stance shows combat readiness

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: homing pigeon animation next to subordinate captain command icon; animation intensity or speed indicates lag magnitude
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: distant captains show delayed command execution; orders take longer to reach distant armies

environmental_storytelling.example: Specific towns provide unique equipment types; only one or two towns on map produce valuable items, making these locations strategically important and visually identifiable through production activity
Visible deforestation in regions directly causes weather system change (increased precipitation), showing environmental cause-effect that impacts gameplay and movement speed
Individual townspeople display name, sex, age, allegiance, vital stats, hometown, and equipment through query tool; population visuals reflect town allegiance and military readiness
Larger towns visually display neutral captains; town size correlates with strategic importance and resource output
Townspeople visually perform jobs (fishing, farming, shepherding, wood collection, crafting) without player direction, establishing living economy and settlement autonomy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: performs assigned job (fishing, farming, shepherding, wood collecting, crafting) visually; displays individual identity (name, sex, age) when queried
follows captain orders; visually equipped with weapons from assigned equipment pile in priority order
follows enemy captain's movements and commands; displays enemy allegiance color; engages in combat when encountering player forces
main character avatar on map; no command lag; can draft soldiers, order equipment invention, manage army posture
displays homing pigeon lag indicator; command execution delayed by distance from player main character; carries excess equipment inventory
controls enemy army; must be killed to eliminate opposing power; if neutral captain survives battle, comes under player command
sheep visible on map; can be slaughtered by player for food resource; represents mobile food supply

notes: PowerMonger uses a hybrid 2D/3D visual system where 3D terrain topography supports 2D sprite entities. Faction identity is primarily communicated through color allegiance rather than symbolic markers. World-state is manifested through four key channels: (1) visual environmental changes (deforestation, weather), (2) behavioral shifts in NPC/soldier activity, (3) power balance scale indicator, (4) individual unit state displays. The game's advanced 'artificial life' engine means visual storytelling includes autonomous NPC behavior as world-state expression. Strategic locations (resource towns, captain positions) are identified through gameplay consequence rather than explicit visual markers. Political control is binary (allegiance to player, enemy, or neutral) communicated through color coding and unit behavior.
